title: Grounding Doesn't Pay: A Token-Matched Negative Result on Creative Diversity — paper + replication package
description: A pre-registered, judge-free, token-matched pilot on multi-agent creative diversity. In-context domain grounding helps per proposal (it beats plain repeated sampling by +0.19 to +0.26 clusters per idea, across two encoders) but is never the token-efficient frontier: a cheap arm always dominates it per token, under both pre-registered clustering algorithms and both encoders, because a 6.8x token cost cannot be amortized by an at-most-1.8x achievable cluster gain. Ships the frozen generation (180 records, SHA-256 per record), the pre-registered scorer, encoder/stopword robustness re-scores, both figures from code, a stdlib paper<->data test, and a Merkle-tree + hash-chain provenance seal (scripts/verify_chain.py) that makes tampering evident and re-ties every generation hash to its content. Single model family (Grok-4.20), two creative queries. Paper in English.

creators.name: Flores, Carlos Ulisses
creators.affiliation: Codex Hash Research Laboratory, Codex Hash Ltda., Brazil
